06525.content: PHive uses a generator-centric persistence pattern. The generator discovers annotated model fields, emits a PTypeAdapter, and routes all persistence behavior through a shared PHiveCtx and stateless PHiveHook pipeline. Generated adapters own payload serialization, metadata encoding via the %PVR% delimiter schema, model-level and field-level hook composition, and read/write lifecycle execution. Encryption hooks resolve key material through PhiveMetaRegistry and the seed provider abstraction.

0739c.summary: Flutter monorepo for PHive runtime and generator
0739c.content: The workspace is a Flutter and Dart monorepo built around build_runner, source_gen, Hive CE, and flutter_test. The main packages are phive for runtime types, phive_generator for adapter generation, phive_barrel for reusable hook templates and seed-provider integration, phive_test for integration validation, and example for the demo application. Key runtime types include PHiveCtx, PHiveHook, PTypeAdapter, PHiveRouter, PHiveDynamicRouter, PHiveStaticRouter, PHiveContainerHandle, and PHiveActionException.

08475.summary: PHiveRouter — both routers implemented; BoxCollection-backed static router
08475.content: The consumer layer has been superseded by PHiveRouter. Both router implementations are now complete. PHiveDynamicRouter is the runtime router: one Box<T> per registered type, Box<dynamic> ref stores holding List<String> child-key indexes, runtime registration at any time. PHiveStaticRouter is the initialization-locked router: uses Hive CE BoxCollection so all type and ref boxes live in one logical database (one IndexedDB store on web, one directory on native). All types and refs must be registered before the first ensureOpen() call; BoxCollection.open() is invoked inside ensureOpen() with the complete set of box names and the schema is frozen after that point. On web, IndexedDB may bypass TypeAdapter dispatch so PTypeAdapter hooks (encryption, TTL) require native-side validation for web targets. The active roadmap is PHiveProcessor above the router layer, PHiveRef generator support for annotation-driven static-router config, and router-level scope configuration.

7307d.summary: Static router stores binary payloads inside BoxCollection
7307d.content:
PHiveStaticRouter no longer hands raw Dart objects to BoxCollection on web. It now serializes both primary values and ref payloads through Hive's binary reader/writer pipeline and stores them as primitive base64 strings in CollectionBox entries.

This keeps the BoxCollection one-database layout while preserving adapter-based PHive behavior across web and native targets.

7c2ae.content: Router registration now has a first-class descriptor seam separate from field hooks and outcome policy. PHivePrimaryKey and PHiveRef declare router topology on models, while the generator emits *RouterDescriptor classes that call register<T>() and createRef<T, P>(). This keeps annotations declarative, keeps router registration explicit, and leaves hook processing and result policy as separate layers.

7ed02.content: Hook-driven read failures now carry composable PHiveActionBehavior values on PHiveActionException instead of relying on router registration policy. Hooks detect conditions like TTL expiry and declare behaviors such as deleteEntry and returnNull, while routers execute those storage side effects with backend-specific context. The dynamic router now uses LazyBox for primary storage so per-key reads can catch and apply behaviors instead of failing during eager box open.

8ba1a.summary: Redesign PHive metadata layout to header global/perField model
8ba1a.content: PHive will stop embedding metadata into per-field payloads and will stop preserving compatibility with the legacy %PVR%/%PAR% surfaces. New adapter generation will write a single versioned metadata header with global and perField sections, while field values are always written to Hive as raw typed values. This removes type instability caused by metadata-bearing field wrappers and eliminates duplicated class metadata across fields.
